_comment_purpose: Security-focused ESLint configuration for the Git Worktrees Native VS Code extension.
This config targets TypeScript files and flags patterns that are dangerous in the
context of a VS Code extension that spawns child processes.

rules._comment_eval_family: Prevent code generation from strings — these bypass static analysis and are shell-injection adjacent.
_comment_child_process: ESLint does not have a built-in rule for child_process usage.
Use the following grep commands to manually audit:
# Verify ONLY execFile is used (never exec, execSync, spawn with shell:true):
# Verify execFile is the only child_process API used:
grep -rn 'child_process' src/             # Should only show worktreeCore.ts
If any of the above greps return unexpected results, investigate immediately.
rules._comment_prototype_pollution: Prevent patterns that could lead to prototype pollution.

no-restricted-globals.name: eval
no-restricted-globals.message: eval() is forbidden. Use a safe alternative.

no-restricted-properties.message: Use execFile() instead of exec() to avoid shell injection.
Use execFileSync() instead of execSync() to avoid shell injection.

paths.name: child_process
paths.message: Only execFile and execFileSync are permitted. exec/spawn with shell invocation are forbidden.

rules._comment_general_safety: General safety rules to prevent common mistakes.
